title: ottelia ulvifolia subsp. lancifolia
intro: Ottelia ulvifolia subsp. lancifolia, also known as Lancet-leaf Ottelia, is a sub-species of aquatic plant belonging to the Hydrocharitaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ottelia ulvifolia subsp. lancifolia include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves are also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in various dishes.
